symptoms.symptom_name: Deep perineal aching pain ('sitting on a golf ball'), worse when sitting

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome / chronic non-bacterial prostatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep perineal ache aggravated by sitting is the hallmark symptom of CP-CPPS and pelvic-floor myofascial pain.
Perineal pain also occurs in acute bacterial prostatitis; distinguished by systemic features and urinalysis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome / chronic non-bacterial prostatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Irritative voiding symptoms including dysuria are common in CP-CPPS.
Dysuria is a cardinal lower-UTI symptom; urinalysis differentiates.
Dysuria with perineal pain is compatible with bacterial prostatitis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome / chronic non-bacterial prostatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Frequency and urgency are part of the CP-CPPS irritative symptom complex.

symptoms.symptom_name: Difficulty/straining to void, passing only small amounts

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Voiding difficulty with severe pain mandates active exclusion of retention — a urological emergency; requires bladder examination or scan, unavailable remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome / chronic non-bacterial prostatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Functional voiding difficulty from pelvic-floor spasm is common in CP-CPPS flares, but is a diagnosis of exclusion here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-exertional flare — marked worsening after heavy exercise

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome / chronic non-bacterial prostatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Post-exertional flare of pelvic-floor myofascial / neuro-inflammatory pain is characteristic of CP-CPPS and points away from an acute infective or obstructive cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No urethral discharge and no visible haematuria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of urethral discharge argues against urethritis/STI as the primary cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ineffective simple analgesia (paracetamol gave no relief)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome / chronic non-bacterial prostatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Poor response to paracetamol is consistent with neuro-inflammatory/myofascial pain but is non-specific.
Absence of reported fever lowers the probability of acute bacterial prostatitis, but self-report of an unmeasured temperature is weak and does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Boggy, exquisitely tender, normal-sized prostate without nodules on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome / chronic non-bacterial prostatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A boggy, tender prostate without a discrete nodule is consistent with prostatitis; sealed clinician-only finding, unavailable via telehealth. (A tense/fluctuant or grossly swollen prostate would instead raise bacterial prostatitis/abscess.)

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal, non-tender testes and epididymis on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal, non-tender testes with a normal lie argue strongly against torsion — but this is a sealed clinician examination finding, not obtainable via chat.
A non-palpable, non-percussible bladder excludes gross acute retention — sealed clinician finding, unavailable remotely, which is why retention must be safety-netted in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urine dipstick negative for nitrites and leukocytes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative nitrites and leukocytes on dipstick argue against UTI and bacterial infection.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome / chronic non-bacterial prostatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative urinalysis (and negative mid-stream culture) is required to support the non-bacterial diagnosis — the source note's own disclaimer. Not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Chronic pelvic pain syndrome / chronic non-bacterial prostatitis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Deep perineal ache worse on sitting, irritative voiding symptoms and a post-exertional flare in an adult male form the characteristic CP-CPPS constellation. However, the constellation is a diagnosis of exclusion — it does not by itself exclude bacterial prostatitis, retention or torsion, all of which require examination/urinalysis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urine dipstick and mid-stream urine microscopy/culture (MSU)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Core test to distinguish bacterial infection from non-bacterial CP-CPPS. The source note's diagnosis explicitly 'assumes a negative mid-stream urine culture, which must be confirmed.' Requires in-person collection/testing — cannot be done over chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bladder examination / post-void residual bladder scan
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To confirm or exclude acute urinary retention given the voiding difficulty. Requires clinician examination or a bladder scanner.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Focused genital and digital rectal examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude testicular torsion/epididymo-orchitis and to assess the prostate. Clinician-only, in-person.
Only if any testicular sign emerges on examination — to exclude torsion. Not indicated up front given gradual perineal (non-testicular) pain.
PSA should NOT be measured during an acute prostatitis flare — inflammation transiently elevates it and it does not aid acute management. Ordering it now is an over-investigation error.
